tall stature, disproportionate limbs, joint laxity, chest deformity, lens subluxation or dislocation, flat feet, pectus excavatum or carinatum, mitral valve prolapse, dilation of the ascending aorta, bifid uvula, thin upper lip, single palmar crease, flexible skin, long fingers, short fourth metacarpals, curved fifth fingernails

café-au-lait spots, freckling in axillary and inguinal regions, neurofibromas and plexiform neuromas, optic glioma, Lisch nodules, macrocephaly, short stature, skeletal abnormalities including scoliosis and sphenoid wing dysplasia, learning problems, attention deficit hyperactivity disorder, hypertension, glucose intolerance, gastrointestinal disorders such as peptic ulcers and diverticuli

mental retardation, distinctive facies with prominent ears, broad thumbs, brachydactyly type E, short stature, congenital heart defects, cleft palate, characteristic teeth, growth hormone resistance, sleep apnea, respiratory distress during infancy, recurrent infections, hernia, hirsutism, obstructive jaundice, cryptorchidism, varicocele, testicular tumors, osteopenia, kyphoscoliosis, clubfoot, and other orthopedic issues

hypogonadism, short stature, obesity, cognitive impairments, weak muscle tone, feeding problems, excessive appetite, small penis and underdeveloped breasts, distinct craniofacial features, almond shaped eyes, narrow bridge of the nose, tight foreskin, strabismus, hypersensitivity to cold temperatures, tremulousness, and mild to moderate developmental delays

bowing of legs due to chondroscleroses, widened wrists and ankles, thickening of bones around major joints, short stature, normal intelligence, no neurological symptoms, normal bone age, normal radiographic maturation, normal dental development, normal external genitals, normal pubertal development, normal breast development, normal menstrual cycle, normal thyroid function tests, normal serum calcium levels, normal urinary excretion of collagen types I and II, normal blood chemistry values, normal hematological parameters, normal electroencephalogram findings, normal magnetic resonance imaging studies, negative family history

typical facial appearance characterized by midline defects, cleft lip and palate, coloboma, hypospadias, polydactyly, radial ray malformation, syndactyly, hypothalamic hamartoma, developmental delay, severe psychomotor retardation, microcephaly, hydrocephalus, brain stem signs, seizures, sensorineural hearing loss, cataracts, cryptochrism, esoteric body odour, eczema, ichthyotic scaling, palmoplantar keratoderma, and various degrees of liver dysfunction

triangular face, deep-set eyes, choanal atresia, posterior embryootoxon, butterfly vertebrae, peripheral pulmonic stenosis, xanthomas, elevated transaminases, pruritus, gallstones, vitamin K–dependent coagulation factor deficiencies, fatty liver, portal hypertension, splenomegaly, absent or severely reduced olfactory bulbs, developmental delay, and variable degree of intellectual disability

wide forehead, frontal bossing, beaked nose, depressed nasal tip, anteverted nostrils, maxillary hypoplasia, mandibular prognathism, micrognathia, retrognathia, open bite, overjet, crossbites, crowding, impacted third molars, hypodontia, peg laterals, clinodactyly, camptodactyly, hallux valgus, hammer toes, pes cavus, talipes equinus, scoliosis, lordosis, kyphosis, platyspondyly, platybasia, basilar invagination, occipitofrontalis synostosis, sagittal sinus thrombosis, intracranial calcifications, developmental delay, and intellectual disability
